diṭṭhādīhi.meaning: because of the seen and so on; with what is seen and the rest
breakdown: Beginning with, and so on. Sandhi: diṭṭha + ādi > diṭṭhādi.
Instrumental/ablative plural ending; also echoes hi, because.

dosakkhayo.meaning: the destruction of ill will; the extinction of hatred
breakdown: Destruction, extinction (the doubled kk is sandhi doubling).

dosasañhitaṁ.meaning: connected with fault; bound up with corruption, blemish
breakdown: Fault, corruption, blemish; also ill will.
Connected with, accompanied by (past participle of sandahati).

dosavinayapariyosānā.meaning: culminating in the driving away of ill will
dosavinayapariyosānā.summary: Parallel in form to mohavinayapariyosāna, culminating in the driving away of illusion.
dosavinayapariyosānā.gram: neuter compound noun, plural or ablative singular

doso.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, from dussati (√dus)
breakdown: Root meaning to spoil, offend, hate.
doso.goenka: Doso, ill will or hatred, is one of the three root defilements together with lobha (craving) and moha (delusion). In practice it manifests as aversion — the mental reaction of pushing away unpleasant experience — and its destruction (dosakkhaya) is part of the goal of the path.

dubbalakhādikā.meaning: eating up the weak; preying on those of poor strength
breakdown: Prefix meaning bad, poor (the doubled bb is sandhi doubling).

dubbalikaraṇītveva.meaning: "...making weak", thus indeed; (called) a weakener, so it is said
dubbalikaraṇītveva.gram: feminine compound noun fused with the quotation phrase tveva (ti + eva)
breakdown: Making, causing: that which makes weak.
Sandhi of ti (quotation marker) + eva (indeed): "thus indeed it is called".

dubbaṇṇo.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from vaṇṇa
breakdown: Complexion, colour, appearance (√var). Sandhi: dur + vaṇṇa > dubbaṇṇa.

dubbhikkhaṁ.summary: Listed in the source alongside roga (disease) and amanussa (non-human spirits) as calamities.
dubbhikkhaṁ.gram: neuter noun, nominative/accusative singular, from bhikkhā
breakdown: Alms, begged food (√bhikkh). Sandhi: dur + bhikkhā > dubbhikkha: when alms are hard to get, famine.
Spoken (past participle of bhāsati, to speak). Sandhi: dur + bhāsita > dubbhāsita.

duccaritaṁ.gram: adjective/neuter noun, nominative/accusative singular, from carita (√car)
breakdown: Conduct, behaviour (past participle of carati, to walk, fare). Sandhi: dur + carita > duccarita.

duddasiko.meaning: ugly to look at; ill-favoured in appearance
breakdown: Looking, of appearance (from √dis, to see). Sandhi: dur + dasika > duddasika.

duggatiṁ.meaning: a bad destination; a woeful state of rebirth
duggatiṁ.gram: feminine abstract noun, accusative singular, from gati (√gam)
breakdown: Going, destination, course of rebirth (√gam + ti). Sandhi: dur + gati > duggati.

dukkaṭakammakārī.meaning: doing wrong deeds; a doer of evil actions
breakdown: Wrongly done, wrong (du(r)- bad + kaṭa, done).

dukkha.meaning: suffering; unsatisfactoriness; the inherent unsatisfactoriness of conditioned existence
breakdown: Traditionally linked to the nave-hole of a wheel or to space: that which runs badly, is hard to bear.
dukkha.goenka: Dukkha is suffering and, more deeply, the inherent unsatisfactoriness of all conditioned existence. Klaus's Volume 1 glossary gives "dukkho — suffering" directly, and Volume 2 introduces dukkha as one of the three basic characteristics of existence: impermanence (anicca), unsatisfactoriness (dukkha) and non-self (anattā). The term anchors the framework of the Four Noble Truths, elaborated in the compounds dukkhasamudayo (the origin of dukkha), dukkhanirodho (the eradication of dukkha), dukkhanirodhagāminī (the way leading to the eradication of dukkha) and dukkhassantaṁ (the end of dukkha). In the tradition, dukkha is not a doctrine to be believed but a reality to be observed directly within the framework of one's own body and mind.

dukkhamacceti.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; accusative noun + 3rd person singular present

dukkhamagama.meaning: not coming into connection with misery
dukkhamagama.gram: verse phrase; accusative noun + verb of motion
breakdown: Went, came (aorist of gacchati); in context, of not meeting with misery.
dukkhamagama.note: uncertain: the lexicon glosses the phrase contextually as "not coming into connection with misery"; the negative sense comes from the surrounding verse.

dukkhamanveti.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; noun + 3rd person singular present

dukkhamiccheyya.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; accusative noun + 3rd person singular optative of icchati

dukkhanirodhagāminiyā.meaning: of the (way) leading to the eradication of suffering
breakdown: Leading to, going to (feminine genitive singular of gāmī).

dukkhanirodhagāminī.meaning: leading to the eradication of suffering (said of the path, the fourth Noble Truth)
breakdown: Leading to, going to (feminine of gāmī, from √gam).
dukkhanirodhagāminī.goenka: Dukkhanirodhagāminī (paṭipadā) is the way of walking the path leading to the eradication of suffering — the fourth Noble Truth, the Noble Eightfold Path. In the tradition's framing the truths are dukkha, dukkhasamudayo (its origin), dukkhanirodho (its eradication) and dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā (the way leading to its eradication).

dukkhanirodhe.meaning: in (regard to) the eradication of suffering

dukkhanirodho.meaning: the eradication of suffering; the cessation of dukkha (the third Noble Truth)

dukkhan­.meaning: (fragment of) dukkhanirodhagāminiyā, of the way leading to the eradication of suffering
dukkhan­.gram: line-break fragment of a compound noun
dukkhan­.note: uncertain: the key is a hyphenation artifact (dukkhan-irodhagāminiyā split across a line); see dukkhanirodhagāminiyā.

dukkhapaṭikkūlo.meaning: averse to pain; finding misery disagreeable

dukkhappaṭikūlo.meaning: averse to misery; finding pain disagreeable
dukkhappaṭikūlo.gram: compound adjective, masculine nominative singular (variant spelling of dukkhapaṭikkūlo)
breakdown: Disagreeable, averse (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).

dukkhasaccaniddeso.meaning: the exposition of the truth of suffering (a section title)

dukkhasamudaye.meaning: in (regard to) the arising of suffering

dukkhasamudayo.meaning: the arising of suffering; the origination of dukkha (the second Noble Truth)

dukkhassantaṁ.gram: compound phrase: genitive noun + masculine noun, accusative singular
breakdown: End, conclusion (accusative). Sandhi: dukkhassa + antaṁ > dukkhassantaṁ.

dukkhavipākan.meaning: resulting in misery; having suffering as its fruit

breakdown: Yielding, producing as outcome. Sandhi: dukkha + udraya > dukkhudraya.

dukkhūpasamagāminaṁ.meaning: leading to the calming of suffering; the way to the appeasement of dukkha
breakdown: Appeasing, calming, allaying. Sandhi: dukkha + upasama > dukkhūpasama.

dullabhasuttaṁ.meaning: the Dullabha Sutta, the Discourse on Those Rare to Encounter
dullabhasuttaṁ.summary: The sutta refers to the three individuals that are rare to encounter in the world.
breakdown: Rare, hard to obtain (du(r)- difficult + labha, obtaining).

dullabho.meaning: rare; hard to obtain; difficult of attainment
dullabho.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from labha (√labh)
breakdown: Attainment, acquisition (√labh). Sandhi: dur + labha > dullabha.

dunnibbeṭho.meaning: difficult to unravel; hard to refute or disentangle
breakdown: Unwinding, unravelling, rebuttal (ni(r) + √veṭh, to twist). Sandhi: dur + nibbeṭha > dunnibbeṭha.

duppadhaṁsiyo.meaning: difficult to destroy; hard to assail
breakdown: To be destroyed, assailable (pa + √dhaṁs + iya). Sandhi doubling: dur + pa- > duppa-.

duppasaho.meaning: difficult to overcome; hard to subdue
breakdown: Overcoming, subduing (pa + √sah). Sandhi doubling: dur + pa- > duppa-.

duppañño.meaning: of weak wisdom; lacking insight; foolish
duppañño.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from paññā (√ñā)
breakdown: Wisdom (paññā: pa + √ñā). Sandhi doubling: dur + pa- > duppa-.

duppaṭinissaggī.meaning: difficult to forsake; hard to let go of
duppaṭinissaggī.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from paṭinissaggī (√sajj)
breakdown: Forsaking, letting loose, relinquishing (paṭi + ni(s) + √sajj). Sandhi doubling: dur + pa- > duppa-.

duranubodho.meaning: difficult to comprehend; hard to understand
duranubodho.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from anubodha (√budh)

duranugama.meaning: difficult to follow; hard to be traced

duruttāni.gram: adjective/neuter noun, nominative/accusative plural, from utta (√vac)
breakdown: Spoken (past participle of √vac > utta), plural: things badly said.

durāsado.meaning: difficult to approach; hard to offend or lay hands on
durāsado.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from āsada (√sad)

breakdown: Root meaning to spoil, offend, corrupt.
Present stem dussa- with 3rd person singular ending.

dussīlo.meaning: of bad morality; immoral; weak in virtue
dussīlo.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from sīla (√sīl)
breakdown: Morality, virtue (sīla). Sandhi: dur + sīla > dussīla.

dutiyabrahmacariyasuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Brahmacariya Sutta, the Second Discourse on Leading the Holy Life
breakdown: Leading the holy life, the pure life of practice.

dutiyabrahmaññasuttaṁ.meaning: the Second Brahmañña Sutta, the Second Discourse on the True Brahmin State
breakdown: The state of a (true) brahmin; the holy state.
